title: Peter Garnrys bedste aktie: Et tip på kontoret gav tidoblet gevinst
summary_md:
# Peter Garnrys bedste aktie: Et tip på kontoret gav tidoblet gevinst

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-05-04  
**Market Sentiment**: Positive

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Investor Peter Garnry, former Saxo Bank chief strategist now running the concentrated, long-term Garnry Capital fund, walks through his personal portfolio philosophy: growth-and-tech orientation (Philip Fisher style) over classic value, high risk tolerance, focus on management quality and durable compounders. He shares conviction holdings (Novo, Kongsberg, ASML, Nubank, Coupang) and lessons from his best (MarketAxess, a 10-bagger) and worst (a hearing-biotech, Boliden) trades. The overall message is bullish on tech, electrification and European defense, with patience framed as the investor's key edge.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 NOVO-B — Novo Nordisk | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Low expectations priced in after a crisis; management turnaround under new CEO; stronger ex-US brand than Lilly could drive a repricing.

#### Reasoning

Garnry argues the market embeds almost no growth expectations in Novo after its crisis, so even modest execution improvement triggers a sharp repricing. He believes the real battle vs Eli Lilly will be won outside the US, where he sees Novo's brand and access as more credible and durable. Held from a low entry through the full up- and down-cycle, giving conviction to hold long-term.

#### Sell Triggers

- if new management fails to execute the turnaround
- if pipeline/historic track record deteriorates
- if Novo loses the ex-US brand and access advantage to Lilly

> *"Så i den här saga är jag mer till The Underdog lige nu. För att den har kommit så långt ned. Det är inte någon nämnevärdig växtförväntning till Novo på det här nivån."*

---

### ⛔ LLY — Eli Lilly | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Good product but expectations are 'sky-high', requiring near-perfect execution; valuation leaves little upside versus Novo.

#### Reasoning

Garnry views the market as efficient and says Lilly's share price already embeds extremely high expectations, meaning it must deliver to perfection with no missteps. Because so much is priced in, he sees worse risk-reward than Novo and prefers the underdog. Not a knock on the product, which he acknowledges is strong, but a valuation/expectations call.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Lilly's valuation/expectations reset lower
- if it builds a clear ex-US brand advantage over Novo

> *"förväntningarna till Lili eller Lili är trånhöj. Alltså de ska nästan leverera till perfektion. De måste inte göra någon fejltrin härifrån."*

---

### 🟢 KOG — Kongsberg Gruppen | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 80%

Core fund holding; strong Norwegian corporate governance and defense/maritime tech aligned with European rearmament and Arctic/Baltic needs.

#### Reasoning

Garnry has a clear affection for Kongsberg: good corporate governance typical of Norwegian firms, plus exactly the technology he expects Europe to need in the Baltic, Arctic, air defense and satellite communications. He frames European defense rearmament as possibly the most important 10-year theme in Europe, with budgets that are still being under-estimated, making Kongsberg a long-term conviction holding despite a 20% drop on a business split.

#### Sell Triggers

- if corporate governance deteriorates
- if European defense budgets fail to expand as expected
- if its technology loses relevance to Europe's defense needs

> *"Jag har så en kärlighet för Kongsberg... de har all den teknologi vi ska brå i Östersöen och i Arktis, och till forsvar av luften och satellitkommunikation."*

---

### 🟢 CELH — Celsius Holdings | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

High-growth energy-drink firm; fell 31% over two days but thesis unchanged after deep re-check, so the fund increased its position.

#### Reasoning

After Celsius dropped 31% in two days post-earnings, Garnry returned to the case, dug deeper and found nothing fundamentally changed, concluding the sell-off was noise rather than a thesis break. The fund used the weakness to add to the position, and the stock has since recovered well. Reflects his discipline of reacting to large drops by re-examining the thesis rather than panicking.

#### Sell Triggers

- if growth materially decelerates
- if the fundamental thesis is found to have changed on deeper review

> *"Där valde vi så att öka investeringen lite."*

---

### 🟢 NU — Nubank | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 75%

Described as the fund's single largest position; a Brazilian bank Garnry analyzes in isolation without external opinions.

#### Reasoning

Garnry singles out Nubank as the largest holding in his concentrated fund, signaling high conviction. He stresses analyzing it on his own, deliberately avoiding others' views to keep an isolated, individual understanding of the company. Consistent with his concentrated, deep-research, long-term approach to a small number of high-quality compounders.

#### Sell Triggers

- if his independent analysis of the company's fundamentals deteriorates
- if management quality declines

> *"Ta den största sällskapen i vår portfölj. Det är Nubank. En stor bank i Brasilien."*

---

### 🟢 ASML — ASML | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 75%

European 'crown jewel' supplying advanced EUV lithography essential to AI/semiconductor chips; held as a core tech position.

#### Reasoning

Garnry calls ASML a European crown jewel and 'superb company', highlighting its near-monopoly on advanced EUV lithography machines critical to producing AI and other chips. It fits his thesis of owning differentiated companies tied to the future that grow faster than the broader economy, and he intends to keep investing in tech.

#### Sell Triggers

- if it loses its lithography technology lead
- if semiconductor/AI demand structurally weakens

> *"AMCML som ju är den här europeiska kronjuvel kan man väl gott kalla det... Så det är ett superfett sällskap."*

---

### 🟡 ADBE — Adobe | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Highly profitable software firm beaten down on the AI-disruption narrative; a poor recent investment he chooses to hold.

#### Reasoning

Garnry concedes Adobe has been a poor investment lately, hit hard by the market narrative that AI will damage many legacy software companies. He notes this disruption has not actually happened yet, and despite the share price weakness he plans to hold ('there you should hold on'). A case where he stays the course pending evidence the AI threat materializes.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI demonstrably erodes Adobe's software franchise
- if profitability/cash generation declines

> *"men aktien blev med i alla fall. Så det är sånt här. Okej, där ska man hålla fast."*

---

### 🟢 MEDP — MedPace | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Clinical research organization outsourcing trials for big pharma; held as tech tilted toward healthcare with a strong platform.

#### Reasoning

Garnry holds MedPace as a CRO that pharma companies like Novo can outsource clinical research to, praising its strong platform. He frames it as technology leaning toward the healthcare sector, fitting his preference for differentiated, growth-oriented businesses serving structural demand in pharma development.

#### Sell Triggers

- if its CRO platform loses competitiveness
- if pharma outsourcing demand declines

> *"ett sällskap som heter MedPace. Som egentligen är en klinisk research-organisation... De har en mycket fin plattform till det."*

---

### 🟢 CPNG — Coupang | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 65%

South Korean e-commerce leader with dense small-warehouse logistics and an internationalization push; private holding he follows closely.

#### Reasoning

Garnry admires Coupang's distinctive dense network of small logistics centers enabling very fast delivery in densely populated South Korea, calling it a fantastic business with a 'super cool' management he watches intently. He notes it is now internationalizing beyond Korea. The stock fell sharply in the 2022 tech sell-off but has started moving positively over the past year.

#### Sell Triggers

- if management quality deteriorates
- if international expansion fails
- if its logistics cost advantage erodes

> *"Så det är ett sällskap jag följer rätt spänt. Och jag syns igen att ledelsen är supercool."*

---

### 🟢 ZAP — Zaptec | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Norwegian EV-charging platform held privately as a speculative bet on electrification; likes management but flags higher risk.

#### Reasoning

Garnry holds Zaptec privately as a more speculative play on electrification, describing it as a fundamentally good company with management he likes. He explicitly notes it is smaller and carries more uncertainty and risk than the fund's holdings, framing it as a higher-risk bet on the EV-charging trend rather than a core conviction position.

#### Sell Triggers

- if electrification adoption slows
- if management execution disappoints
- if competition intensifies in EV charging

> *"för exempel har jag investerat i Zaptek i Norge, privat... Och det är egentligen bara ett bet på elektrifiering."*

---

### 🟢 TW — Tradeweb | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 75%

Thomson Reuters spin-off; Garnry judged it a better company, management and product than MarketAxess and switched into it.

#### Reasoning

After recognizing that competitor Tradeweb had become markedly better than his long-held MarketAxess on every parameter, Garnry updated his view, sold MarketAxess and bought Tradeweb. He still likes the structural theme of bond trading moving electronic, but now backs Tradeweb as the superior franchise and management within that trend.

#### Sell Triggers

- if a stronger competitor surpasses it as Tradeweb did to MarketAxess
- if the electronic-bond-trading thesis breaks
- if management quality declines

> *"Att Tradeweb är faktiskt ett bättre sällskap och det är en bättre ledelse och det är ett bättre produkt. än Market Access."*

---

### 🔴 MKTX — MarketAxess | **SELL** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

His best-ever ~10x trade from 2012, but he sold after concluding competitor Tradeweb had become superior on all parameters.

#### Reasoning

Garnry rode MarketAxess from ~2012 as a bond-trading compounder for roughly a 10x gain, but the stock began falling and he realized a stronger competitor, Tradeweb, was beating it across the board. He updated his thesis, exited MarketAxess after it had already pulled back meaningfully from its top, and rotated the capital into Tradeweb.

#### Sell Triggers

- if it regains a competitive edge over Tradeweb (would reverse the sell)
- thesis already invalidated by superior competitor

> *"Så sålde jag Market Access. Och den hade tagit ett pängt dyck från toppen."*

---

### 🟠 BE — Bloom Energy | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Fuel-cell maker generating on-site power (relevant to AI data centers); had big success but Garnry exited too early and now watches electrification names.

#### Reasoning

Garnry held Bloom Energy, a fuel-cell company whose on-site 'server-like' power generation is attractive for new AI data centers, but admits he sold far too early before its big run. He is now actively searching for the next electrification opportunity to own for the coming decade, spending significant time on the theme, suggesting continued interest rather than a fresh position.

#### Sell Triggers

- if fuel-cell economics fail to scale
- if AI data-center power demand thesis weakens

> *"Och det är ett firma som heter Bloom Energy som laver sådana här bränsleceller... Men där kom jag ut för tidligt."*

---

### 🟡 ENR — Siemens Energy | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Held privately in the electrification theme for a nice gain but exited; annoyed by its weaker wind-turbine division.

#### Reasoning

Garnry was invested in Siemens Energy as part of his electrification thesis and took a nice gain, but was irritated by its wind-turbine division being not 'super strong'. He frames it as a past position he has moved on from while hunting for the next, cleaner electrification play, so conviction is limited.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the wind-turbine division continues to underperform
- already partly exited the position

> *"Siemens Energy var jag inne. Fick också en fin gevinst. Var bara irriterad över att de har den här vindmölldivision som inte är super super stark."*

---

### 🟠 SAAB-B — Saab | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Neutral | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Interesting defense name within the European rearmament theme but not owned personally or professionally.

#### Reasoning

Asked about Saab, Garnry says he owns it neither professionally nor personally but finds it a fairly interesting company within the broader European defense rearmament theme he favors. He prefers Kongsberg, so Saab is a watch-level name rather than a conviction buy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if European defense spending fails to expand
- if it lacks the specific capabilities Europe needs

> *"Nej, det har jag inte, varken professionellt eller personligt, men jag syns att Saab är ett rätt intressant sällskap."*

---

### ⛔ RHM — Rheinmetall | **AVOID** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Neutral | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Interesting as Germany spends heavily, but Garnry feels it lacks the specific capabilities he expects Europe's defense to need; not owned.

#### Reasoning

Garnry finds Rheinmetall interesting because Germany is spending heavily and will favor its domestic champion, but says it doesn't have as much of what he expects Europe to actually need in its defense buildup. He does not own it and prefers Kongsberg's technology mix, making this a soft avoid relative to peers.

#### Sell Triggers

- if it develops the defense capabilities Europe needs
- if German procurement shifts decisively in its favor (would reverse view)

> *"Men jag syns inte att regnmetall har lika mycket av de ting som jag förväntar att vi ska brå i EUPs forsvar."*

---
